solution.p: Lets start by noting that -1 is a common factor to each term, so we may write

To factor this expression further we make the following observations:
the leading coefficient is 1
the last term is negative which means that the factorization will have one of the following forms
(x-\phantom{3})(x+\phantom{3})\qquad (x+\phantom{3})(x-\phantom{3})
We look for factors of -45 that add to -4; the factors that work are -9 and 5 so
Note that this answer can be checked by using the FOIL (First Outside Inside Last) technique (exercise).
